content_blocks.text:
get to know the Java API 
you are here
133
Some things you can do with ArrayList
1
Make one
ArrayList<Egg> myList = new ArrayList<Egg>();
2
Put something in it
Egg 5 = new Egg();
myList.add(5);
7
Remove something from it
myList.remove(5);
4
Find out how many things are in it
int theSize = myList.size();
5
Find out if it contains something
boolean isIn = myList.contains(5);
6
Find out if it’5 empty
boolean empty = myList.isEmpty();
Find out where something is (i.e. its index)
int idx = myList.indexOf(b);
8
3
Put another thing in it
Egg b = new Egg();
myList.add(b);
A new ArrayList object is 
created on the heap. It’5 little 
because it’5 empty. 
Now the ArrayList grows a “box” 
to hold the Egg object.
The ArrayList grows again to hold 
the second Egg object.
The ArrayList is holding 2 objects 50 
the size() method returns 2
The ArrayList DOES contain the Egg object 
referenced by ‘5’, 50 contains() returns true
ArrayList is zero-based (means first index is 0) 
and since the object referenced by ‘b’ was the 
second thing in the list, indexOf() returns 1
it’5 definitely NOT empty, 50 isEmpty() 
returns false
Hey look — it shrank!
Don’t worry about this new <Egg> angle-bracket syntax 
right now; it just means “make this a list of Egg objects”.
5
5
b
b

you are here
133
Some things you can do with ArrayList
1
Make one
ArrayList<Egg> myList = new ArrayList<Egg>();
2

myList.add(5);
7

4

Now the ArrayList grows a “box” 
to hold the Egg object.
